https://www.reuters.com/sports/basketbal.summary: - Kawhi Leonard scored 32 points, grabbed 7 rebounds, and had 5 assists as the Los Angeles Clippers overwhelmed the Indiana Pacers 123-97.

https://www.gov.uk/employment-tribunal-d.summary:
- The Employment Tribunal found in favour of the claimants, Ms R Graham and others, that they were unfairly dismissed.
- The tribunal concluded that the claimants' employment was terminated in breach of contract.
- The decision was made after considering evidence from both parties, including witness statements and documentary evidence.
- The claimants were awarded compensation and the tribunal ordered the respondents to pay the claimants' costs.

https:///en/press_release_detail.html?id.summary:
- Total port cargo throughput increased by 1.1% to 44.8 million tonnes comparing Q4 2025 with the same period a year earlier.
- Comparing Q4 2025 with a year earlier, no further information provided.

- A Hong Kong family's daily life is used as an example to understand GDP (Gross Domestic Product) in a new blog article.
- The article aims to help readers grasp the concept of GDP in a more relatable and tangible way.
- The story follows a family's expenses and income to illustrate the concept of GDP.
- The article provides a fresh perspective on understanding GDP and its relevance to everyday life.
- The value of total retail sales in January 2026 increased by 5.5% over a year earlier.

https://www.gov.uk/employment-tribunal-d.summary:
- The Employment Tribunal found that Mr B Singh was unfairly dismissed by the Metropolitan Police Service.
- The tribunal ruled that Mr Singh was subjected to harassment and bullying by his colleagues, which contributed to his eventual dismissal.
- The Commissioner of Police of the Metropolis was held liable for the actions of Mr Singh's colleagues.
- The tribunal awarded Mr Singh compensation for his unfair dismissal.

https://www.reuters.com/world/africa/som.summary:
- The Somali parliament has approved a constitutional change that extends the president's term in office.
- The move delays the 2022 presidential election to 2026.
- The change was approved by 252 lawmakers, exceeding the required two-thirds majority.
- The new term will run from 2026 to 2032.

https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/s.summary:
- Despite making up 43% of the agricultural workforce, women farmers often lack access to resources, training, and credit, hindering their ability to adapt to climate change.
- Women farmers are more likely to prioritize climate-resilient practices, such as crop rotation and agroforestry, which benefit the environment and improve food security.
- Empowering women farmers can have a multiplier effect, benefiting entire communities and ecosystems.
- The European Commission's MAMAN (Mainstreaming Agriculture in the Middle East and North Africa) initiative aims to support women farmers in the region, recognizing their critical role in achieving climate goals.

https://www.gov.uk/government/news/aaib-.summary:
- Loss of multiple flight deck displays during climb 14 nm north-east of Aberdeen Airport on 27 August 2022
- The incident involved an ATR 42-500 aircraft with the registration G-LMRC

https://www.gov.uk/government/news/repor.summary:
- The accident occurred on 28 March 2025 at Ickenham London Underground station.
- RAIB investigated the incident.
- The report has been released.

https://www.gov.uk/government/news/baron.summary:
- Baroness Maggie Jones of Whitchurch has been appointed as the new Chair of Acas.
- The appointment followed a fair and open public appointment process.

https://www.reuters.com/business/finance.summary:
- EU's economic affairs commissioner Olli Rehn says the European Central Bank should not be too optimistic about an early end to the war in Iran.
- Rehn says the war's impact on the global economy is still uncertain.
- He warns that the conflict could lead to higher oil prices and inflation.
- Rehn also says the EU should maintain its sanctions on Iran until the country addresses international concerns about its nuclear program.

https://www.reuters.com/business/toronto.summary:
- Toronto home sales fell to a 10-month low in February, with 6,736 transactions recorded.
- The decline is attributed to the ongoing trade war and rising interest rates.
- Sales were down 16.4% compared to the same month last year.
- The average price of a Toronto home was $930,000, a 2.3% increase from February 2022.
